title: Ship Download as PDF button on Sidecar Capital Partners blog posts
narrative: Add a 'Download as PDF' button at the top of every published blog post on sidecarcapitalpartners.com. Button triggers window.print(); PDF is generated live from current post content (no stale cached files). Site is WordPress.com Atomic; WPCode Lite already installed — no new plugins. Pagination/typography/branding controlled by @media print stylesheet (page-break-after: avoid on headings, orphans/widows 3 on paragraphs, page-break-inside: avoid on images/tables/blockquotes/pre/figure). Two steps. Step 1 — Add print CSS via WP Admin → Appearance → Customize → Additional CSS → Publish. CSS hides site chrome (header/footer/nav, .site-header, .site-footer, .wp-block-navigation, .sidebar, .widget-area, .comments-area, #comments, .wp-block-post-comments-form, .related-posts, .jp-relatedposts, .sharedaddy, .sd-sharing-enabled, .wpfront-notification-bar, #sidecar-pdf-btn, .skip-link). @page sets 0.75in/0.6in margins with branded headers/footers: top-left 'Sidecar Field Notes', top-right 'sidecarcapitalpartners.com', bottom-right 'Page N of M', bottom-left '© Sidecar Capital Partners' — all Georgia 9pt teal (#002229). Typography: Georgia 11pt/1.55, headings teal (#002229) at 22/15/12pt. Links print with inline URL via a[href^='http']:after (skip mailto and anchors). Full-width content in print. Button styled teal (#002229), white text, inline-flex, 10px 20px padding, 4px radius, hover #003d47. Step 2 — Add button via WPCode. WP Admin → Code Snippets → Add Snippet → Add Your Custom Code. Title: 'Sidecar PDF Download Button'. Code Type: HTML Snippet. Location: Insert Before Content. Auto Insert: on. Smart Conditional Logic: Page Type → Is → Single Post (blog posts only, not homepage/about/category). Paste: . Save and Activate. Test: open any published blog post, confirm teal '↓ Download as PDF' button at top, click triggers print dialog, Save as PDF shows clean Georgia typography with teal headings, no nav/sidebar/comments/share/button chrome, 'Sidecar Field Notes' top-left, URL top-right, page numbers and copyright in footer, headings not orphaned, images/blockquotes don't split across pages. Once live, send Jeremy a link to the test post so he can sanity-check pagination and formatting on real content.

title: Investigate why CLI task updates aren't pushing to origin/main
narrative: After 4/17 catch-up bug (cloud session missed CLI updates), cloud-side fix was shipped at 21:57 ET (commit 9683a2c — boot-sync Phase A/B split forces workspace refresh). Jeremy tested again at ~22:20 ET and the cloud catch-up still didn't reflect his CLI updates. Check of origin/main: no CLI commits (non-cloud-prefixed, -0400 TZ) since b233d56 at 17:53 ET on 4/17 — so CLI isn't reaching GitHub at all. Write side is broken, not read side. Likely culprits to investigate: (1) CLI agent is committing locally but never running git push (CLAUDE.md says 'commit and push' but there's no explicit push instruction in the task-update flow, and boot-sync step 8 explicitly says 'Do not push — the commit will go out with whatever work comes next', which means if the task-update turn only runs boot-sync's cache write and doesn't add its own push, nothing ships); (2) CLI is pushing to a non-main branch or non-origin remote; (3) CLI turns are erroring silently on push and Jeremy doesn't see it. Action: on next CLI session, before making any task updates, run `git log origin/main..main --oneline` and `git status` to inventory any unpushed commits sitting on the desktop. If unpushed commits exist, push them manually and then fix the prompt path so future task updates push atomically. Also audit CLAUDE.md + prompts/ for the exact place where the CLI write flow was supposed to push — tighten the instruction to make push explicit and unconditional on every state-change turn.
Jeremy confirmed done 4/17 during late-evening cloud catch-up. Cache showed status=open due to 5:14 PM sync cutoff + suspected CLI push bug (see queued PA-7 investigation task).
